name: DS18B20 Temperature Sensor
tagline: Accurate waterproof digital temperature sensor using a single-wire 1-Wire protocol
description: The DS18B20 is a digital temperature sensor that communicates over the Dallas 1-Wire protocol, requiring only a single data wire (plus GND and VCC). It measures temperature from −55°C to +125°C with ±0.5°C accuracy in the −10°C to +85°C range. The waterproof probe version is perfect for liquid and outdoor temperature monitoring. Multiple DS18B20 sensors can share a single Arduino pin using different 64-bit addresses.

pinout.name: Data
pinout.desc: 1-Wire data line — connect to Arduino digital pin via 4.7kΩ pull-up to VCC

pinout.name: VCC
pinout.desc: Power supply 3.3V–5V (or parasitic power via data line)

wiringNote: Always add a 4.7kΩ pull-up resistor between the DQ (data) line and VCC. Without it, the 1-Wire bus won't work and you'll get -127°C or NaN readings.
libraryInstall: Arduino IDE → Library Manager → Install 'DallasTemperature' by Miles Burton. OneWire is installed as a dependency automatically.
commonMistakes: Missing 4.7kΩ pull-up resistor on DQ → sensor reads -127°C or garbage
Not calling sensors.requestTemperatures() before getTempCByIndex() → returns stale data
Using wrong wire color mapping on probe version → check: black=GND, red=VCC, yellow=data
